business.headline: Axis Bank appoints ex-Bandhan Bank finance chief Rajeev Mantri as CFOMantri will assume charge on September 28, 2026, subject to the necessary regulatory and statutory approvals, the bank said in a regulatory filing.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

Fake bosses, real losses: Sebi warns of scammers posing as CEOs in rising boss scamSebi has warned companies about a rising 'boss scam' in which fraudsters impersonate senior executives to trick employees into transferring funds, and urged firms to strengthen their security mechanisms and enhance vigilance.

F1 could return to India in 2028 as government races to fix old tax hurdlesThe government has begun talks with stakeholders to revive Formula One in India by 2028. The push centres on fixing old tax and regulatory hurdles that derailed the Indian Grand Prix.

VFS Global continues to invest in AI to streamline visa application processingSpeaking at India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, Yummi Talwar, COO, South Asia, VFS Global, said visa demand has largely returned to normal after the post-pandemic surge.

Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

entertainment.headline: Inside Ramayana Delhi launch: Ranbir as Ram, Yash's grandeur and Sunny's surprise The Ramayana team gathered in Delhi for the trailer launch and Pratham Sankalp ceremony. The event brought the principal cast together as the makers outlined the film's wider cultural and theatrical plans.

Armaan Khera denies Kushal Tandon is running Alliance: Just a piece on chessboard Actor Armaan Khera has dismissed claims that Kushal Tandon is running Alliance and said he is only one piece on the chessboard. He also said the show changed his outlook, even as its politics left him feeling nearly depressed.

How five minutes of Yash's presence stole the Ramayana event in Delhi Yash needed just five minutes to take over the Ramayana Pratham Sankalp event in Delhi. And after watching him as Raavan in the exclusively screened trailer, it is easy to see why the Kannada superstar's presence has left audiences wanting more.

Kartik Aaryan erupts in joy with parents after first National Award win. Video Actor Kartik Aaryan screamed with joy and hugged his parents after winning Best Actor for Chandu Champion at the 72nd National Film Awards. The viral moment captured his family's celebration as he said he was still processing the win.

Rajamouli, Chiranjeevi, Mahesh Babu over the moon as Skyroot's Vikram-1 soars SS Rajamouli, Chiranjeevi and Mahesh Babu congratulated Skyroot on the successful Vikram-1 rocket launch. Their public messages put the spotlight on India's historic space journey.

Katrina Kaif's birthday album with Vicky Kaushal and son Vihaan is pure joy Actor Katrina Kaif shared beautiful moments from her 43rd birthday celebrations with Vicky Kaushal and son Vihaan. The post offered fans a rare look at her low-key family celebration.

National Film Awards: Dhanush, Mammootty celebrate Raayan and Brahmayugam wins Actors Dhanush and Mammootty shared posts on social media after winning major honours at the 72nd National Film Awards. They celebrated their win and congratulated other winners as well.

Sonakshi Sinha, Dia Mirza stand with Sonam Wangchuk, urge peace and unity Sonakshi Sinha and Dia Mirza backed Sonam Wangchuk and the students protesting at Jantar Mantar after police moved him from the site. Their support came as his health intensified calls for accountability, dialogue and education reforms.

Why Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana needs to conquer China to win the box office war Buzz around Ramayana Part 1 exploring the massive China market is growing. If the makers crack the deal, it will take the Ranbir Kapoor film's box office game to a new level.

The Odyssey box office Day 2: Saturday rush takes Nolan film near Rs 40 crore Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey posted a strong jump at the Indian box office on its second day. Positive word-of-mouth, IMAX demand and a solid English-language response kept its momentum intact.

Hrithik Roshan backs Sonam Wangchuk, says students' struggles sound true Actor Hrithik Roshan has extended support to activist Sonam Wangchuk amid his indefinite hunger strike over alleged examination irregularities. The actor shared Wangchuk's message on social media and said students' struggles resonated with him.

Sunny Deol confirms Lord Hanuman role in Ramayana, says he's just started filming Sunny Deol surprised fans at the Ramayana trailer launch and confirmed that he will portray Lord Hanuman in Nitesh Tiwari's epic saga. The actor called the opportunity one of the biggest honours of his career and shared his excitement about bringing the beloved character to life.

Our stars aligned: Jennifer Winget marries William Ishmael, shares wedding glimpses Actor Jennifer Winget got married to Singapore-based businessman William Ishmael in an intimate wedding ceremony. The actor shared dreamy pictures from her special day, giving fans a glimpse of her elegant bridal look and romantic celebration.

72nd National Film Awards full winners list: Best Actor, Best Film and more The 72nd National Film Awards were announced in New Delhi on Saturday, with Article 370 named Best Film. The list also recognised performers and filmmakers across feature and non-feature categories for CBFC-certified 2024 releases.

Choreographer Vijay Ganguly says National Award for Aaj Ki Raat is family milestone Vijay Ganguly has won the National Award for Best Choreography for Aaj Ki Raat from Stree 2. He said the honour is special because it brings a third National Award into his family.

It's a performance for the ages: Kabir Khan on Kartik Aaryan's National Award Director Kabir Khan said he called actor Kartik Aaryan after learning he had won his first National Award for Chandu Champion. In an exclusive interview with India Today, the director said Kartik's physical and mental transformation made the honour feel like a personal win.

72nd National Film Awards: Mammootty, Dhanush and Amaran win big from South The 72nd National Film Awards were announced in New Delhi on July 18. Malayalam superstar Mammootty won Best Actor, while Dhanush's Captain Miller and Sivakarthikeyan's Amaran bagged top nods for the 2024.

National Awards: Article 370 wins Best film, Rajkumar Periasamy is Best Director Article 370 won the Best Feature Film award at the 72nd National Film Awards, while Rajkumar Periasamy was honoured as Best Director for his Tamil war drama Amaran. These accolades highlight the rich storytelling and cinematic excellence celebrated across Indian cinema for the year 2024.

72nd National Awards: Mammootty, Yami Gautam, Kartik Aaryan win Best Actor honours The winners of the 72nd National Film Awards have been announced, with Yami Gautam, Mammootty and Kartik Aaryan bagging top acting honours. The prestigious awards recognised outstanding films and performances from across Indian cinema.

general news.headline: Seat at the table for TMC rebels at all-party meeting, INDIA bloc MPs walk outAfter briefly walking out of the meeting, leaders of the INDIA bloc returned and rejoined the discussions, describing their exit as a symbolic protest against the government's decision to provide separate seating to the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs.

Make Parliament march a success: Sonam Wangchuk's first message from hospitalSonam Wangchuk said from hospital that his removal from Jantar Mantar amounted to illegal detention. He framed the CJP campaign as India's 2nd Freedom Movement seeking freedom from fear and injustice.

Vintage PV Sindhu beats world champion to win Japan Open crown, ends title droughtPV Sindhu beat Akane Yamaguchi to become the first Indian to win the Japan Open. The victory ended her seven-year wait for a major title on the tour. It comes a month before the world championships in New Delhi.

Death to the compromiser: Iran hardliners turn on leadership over US dealHardline factions in Iran have accused senior leaders of staging a soft coup after the US agreement. The charge has sharpened internal divisions as Mojtaba Khamenei remains out of public view.

India's mini-republic townships are selling what Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru cannotIndia's gated townships are like mini-republics. They are not replacing cities, but they are filling the gaps left by failing civic infrastructure. The question they pose is not why people are buying into them, but why so many feel they have to.

Lost faith in hospital: Sonam Wangchuk's wife goes to court over 'illegal detention'Sonam Wangchuk's wife Gitanjali Angmo has moved the Delhi High Court seeking his transfer from Safdarjung Hospital to a private hospital, claiming she has no trust in the government facility. The plea alleges his continued confinement is illegal, seeks an urgent hearing, and demands his immediate release or discharge.

He's a war criminal: What Zohran Mamdani said on arresting Netanyahu in NYCAt the same time, however, Mamdani also admitted he was unsure if he had the legal authority to order the New York Police Department, which he oversees, to take a foreign leader into custody.

Chennai's Amma canteens see 55% surge in footfall after CM Vijay's revamp pushDaily footfall at Chennai's 383 Amma Canteens has risen from 67,164 in April to over 1.04 lakh after the Tamil Nadu government launched a renovation and modernisation drive.

How five minutes of Yash's presence stole the Ramayana event in DelhiYash needed just five minutes to take over the Ramayana Pratham Sankalp event in Delhi. And after watching him as Raavan in the exclusively screened trailer, it is easy to see why the Kannada superstar's presence has left audiences wanting more.

Kerala declares Monday holiday for schools over FIFA World Cup finalKerala has declared a holiday for educational institutions on Monday ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain. Scheduled examinations will continue as planned, following demands for student convenience.

Crocodile strolls into Kota hostel mess, cooks flee in panicA 4-feet-long crocodile entered the basement mess of a boys' hostel in Kota, triggering panic among staff and students. The safe rescue and viral CCTV footage have renewed focus on human-wildlife encounters near residential areas.

71-year-old NEET aspirant asks HC: Why no reservation for senior citizens in MBBS?A 71-year-old NEET UG 2026 aspirant has moved the Allahabad High Court seeking 1% reservation or a special quota for senior citizens in MBBS admissions. Ashok Bahar says the right to education should not end with age as he continues chasing his 52-year-old dream of becoming a doctor.

Wenger's is 100. Delhi has changed. Its appetite for the bakery hasn'tHandcrafted ByOur Editors Wenger's, naam toh suna hi hoga. As the iconic Delhi bakery turns 100, we revisit the stories, celebrities and generations of customers that have made it a part of the capital's cultural memory.

Monsoon set for a strong comeback: When and where will it rain next week?The monsoon is set for a strong comeback early next week, with widespread rain expected over North, East and Central India. A fresh Bay of Bengal low-pressure system will pull the monsoon trough back into position and end the sticky heat.

India wins 4 golds, finishes top at 58th International Chemistry OlympiadIndia won four gold medals at the 58th International Chemistry Olympiad in Tashkent. The result gave the country its best finish yet and a joint top rank.

Hyderabad techie enters temple naked, takes idol, jumps into pond; diesPolice probing the death of a Hyderabad software engineer claim she ran naked to a nearby temple, picked up an idol of Goddess Ammavaru and then jumped into a pond while carrying it.

Tears, heartbreak, history: How Lord's became India women's emotional homeThe Indian women's team has shared a turbulent relationship with Lord's, enduring the heartbreak of the 2017 ODI World Cup final before scripting history with a landmark victory in the first-ever women's Test at the Home of Cricket.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

10 dead, many missing as heavy rain triggers flash floods in J&K's Rajouri, PoonchTorrential rain triggered flash floods and landslides across Rajouri and Poonch, killing at least 10 people, leaving several others missing, damaging homes and vehicles, and forcing large-scale rescue and evacuation operations.

Tulsi Gabbard's brother charged with trying to lure children to his hotel roomBatarti Gabbard has been charged after Honolulu Police said he tried to lure children to his hotel room. The case, described as a "stranger danger" incident, came as his family says he is undergoing treatment.

Do influencers even have a life?Be it Instagram, YouTube or any other social media platform, many influencers seem to feel the pressure to vlog every moment of their day. Pressure, because views. Just for the sake of views. But is it necessary?

Monsoon set to return to Delhi, heavy rain across Himalayan statesDelhi, which has experienced long dry spells interspersed with isolated showers in recent weeks, is expected to see frequent rain beginning Sunday.

51 in maths, IIT, ISRO and rockets: The story of Pawan Kumar ChandanaSaturday was a big day for India's space ambitions. The launch of Vikram-I this morning was successful. Now, the man behind the launch, an IITian, a former ISRO scientist, and an ambitious entrepreneur who gave India's space journey a new direction, has emerged from the ground to become a sensation and an inspiration for many youngsters.

15-hour workdays, 4 months without pay: Hyderabad woman seeks rescue from OmanShabnam Begum from Hyderabad has sought help from the Indian Embassy in Muscat after alleging abuse by her employer in Oman. Her family has sought her safe return and action against the agent who allegedly sent her on false promises.

sports.headline: Spanish Renaissance: How De la Fuente modernised tiki-taka to revive Spain FIFA World Cup 2026 final: Spain stands on the cusp of history as their renaissance in World football is about to come to a full circle. Standing behind the turnaround is Luis de La Fuente, a manager who re-engineered La Roja's tiki-taka DNA, and man-managed his players to bring out their best.

3rd ODI Live Score: Focus on Rohit Sharma as India eye series win India (IND) vs England (ENG), 3rd ODI Live Score: India are aiming to clinch the series at Lord's, but the spotlight will firmly be on Rohit Sharma, who has dominated headlines in recent days amid speculation over his retirement from international cricket.

LAKR win maiden MLC title to leave Shah Rukh Khan ecstatic. Here's what he said Los Angeles Knight Riders beat Washington Freedom by one run to win their first Major League Cricket title. This left Shah Rukh Khan elated as the Knight Riders owner praised his side for their title triumph.

The problem of the spokesperson in Indian cricket Indian cricket's biggest problem may not be selection, succession or retirement. It is that the wrong people keep answering the biggest cricketing questions, while those entrusted with the game's decisions remain conspicuously silent.

Tears, heartbreak, history: How Lord's became India women's emotional home The Indian women's team has shared a turbulent relationship with Lord's, enduring the heartbreak of the 2017 ODI World Cup final before scripting history with a landmark victory in the first-ever women's Test at the Home of Cricket.

Is this Messi's last World Cup game? Emotional post fuels farewell talk Lionel Messi sent an emotional message to Argentina's squad before the World Cup final against Spain. However, fans started to speculate it was his final match in Argentina colours, atleast at the World Cup.

Vintage PV Sindhu beats world champion to win Japan Open crown, ends title drought PV Sindhu beat Akane Yamaguchi to become the first Indian to win the Japan Open. The victory ended her seven-year wait for a major title on the tour. It comes a month before the world championships in New Delhi.

Rohit Sharma eyes defining Lord's innings as retirement rumours grow louder ENG vs IND, 3rd ODI preview: The speculation over Rohit Sharma playing his final ODI at Lord's is not refusing to end, but the Indian team's focus is now shifting to the series decider in London. Here's all that you need to know about the third ODI.

Messi's greatest triumph could come where he once walked away from Argentina Spain vs Argentina Final: In 2016, Messi announced his retirement at MetLife Stadium, devastated by another heartbreak with Argentina. Now a decade later, that same ground could host his greatest moment in international football.

Messi vs Yamal: The World Cup final where Barcelona's past and future collide FIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina and Spain meet in the World Cup final with Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal at the centre of attention. A resurfaced Barcelona photograph and a clash of styles have given the match an added sense of history.

WWE debut, NBA audition. Novak Djokovic leaves fans speechless in New York Novak Djokovic turned Fanatics Fest in New York into his own crossover stage with a WWE-style entrance, clean three-pointers and a UFC power test. Djokovic interviewed Lionel Messi on Day one of the event.

Japan Open final highlights: Sindhu beats world champion Yamaguchi to win titlePV Sindhu vs Akane Yamaguchi highlights, Japan Open 2026 final: PV has become the first Indian shuttler to win the prestigious Japan Open title. It's her first major title in seven years. The Indian shuttler beats world champion Akane Yamaguchi 21-17, 21-17 to clinch the memorable win.

Argentina vs Spain: Will storms or wildfire smoke affect FIFA World Cup final?Canadian wildfire smoke and severe thunderstorms raised concerns ahead of the FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain, but meteorologists believe the same storms disrupting preparations will clear the air, paving the way for favourable conditions at kick-off.

One match won't tarnish Didier Deschamps' legacy: Mbappe as France finish fourthKylian Mbappe admitted France wanted to give Didier Deschamps a winning farewell but said a disastrous first half against England left Les Bleus chasing the game. The captain insisted the defeat would not diminish Deschamps' remarkable legacy.

England's win over France is first step towards football's elite: Thomas TuchelThomas Tuchel believes England's dramatic 6-4 win over France is the first major step towards closing the gap on international football's elite. The England boss praised his players' mentality after securing the World Cup bronze medal.

Too little, too late? England and France save their best for 3rd place thrillerFIFA World Cup 2026: Nobody wanted to play for third place, yet England and France produced the World Cup's most entertaining match. Bukayo Saka's hat-trick sealed a breathless 6-4 win, while Kylian Mbappe rewrote the record books despite finishing on the losing side.

FRA 4-6 ENG Highlights: Bellingham ends goal-fest as England win bronzeFrance vs England Highlights, FIFA World Cup 3rd place play-off: (FRA 4-5 ENG) England defeat France 6-4 in a breathtaking third-place playoff as Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick and Jude Bellingham seals victory in stoppage time.

Should've offered you a better ending but we failed: Mbappe's tribute to DeschampsHours before France's World Cup third-place playoff against England, Kylian Mbappe paid an emotional tribute to outgoing coach Didier Deschamps, saying Les Bleus had "failed" to give him the farewell he deserved after 14 years at the helm.

World Cup: Rooney keeps Haaland's promise, rows across Hudson like a VikingWayne Rooney has finally paid up after losing a playful FIFA World Cup bet to Erling Haaland. The former England captain swapped the BBC studio for a rowing boat on New York's Hudson River, honouring a promise he made after doubting Norway's chances of reaching the quarter-finals.

PV Sindhu in first final in 2 years: When and where to watch Japan OpenPV Sindhu has entered the final of Japan Open. This is Sindhu's first final appearance in two years. Want to know when and where to watch her match against Akane Yamaguchi? Check out all the details here.

Rafael Nadal hopes Spain can relive 2010 magic in World Cup final vs ArgentinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Rafael Nadal has sent Spain an emotional message before the World Cup final against Argentina. His recall of the 2010 triumph has added to the support gathering around Luis de la Fuente's side.

Indian football is in free-fall. How to save it?Former India coach Stephen Constantine has warned that Indian football is in "free-fall", calling for structural reforms and questioning the AIFF's long-term vision after being overlooked for a third stint with the national team.

Retirement drama gone, Rohit and Gambhir share a laugh in Lord's balconyRohit Sharma and Gautam Gambhir were seen sharing a good laugh on the Lord's balcony. Rumours were rife ahead of the 3rd ODI that the Indian team management wanted to move on from Rohit.

India hit by fresh injury setback as Washington Sundar ruled out of Lord's ODIENG vs IND, 3rd ODI: India suffered another injury blow ahead of the series-deciding Lord's ODI after Washington Sundar was ruled out with a hamstring injury sustained in Cardiff.

India's Minerva Academy defend World Youth Cup, complete European doubleDays after conquering the Helsinki Cup, Minerva Academy retained the Gothia Cup by edging Brazilian side RS Sports Yellow 2-1 in another historic triumph.

Mirabai Chanu, Lovlina Borgohain to lead India at CWG 2026 opening ceremonyOlympic medallists Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain will lead India's 126-member contingent at the opening ceremony of the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow on July 23. The honour adds another milestone to the decorated careers of two of India's biggest sporting stars.

You use Donald Trump as a witness? Thomas Tuchel hits back at England tactics digThomas Tuchel dismissed Donald Trump's criticism of his use of Harry Kane before England's third-place play-off. Trump trolled Tuchel for his defensive tactics during the semi-final loss to Argentina.

How a sex party raid nearly ended FIFA World Cup final referee's careerSlavko Vincic is all set for the biggest moment of his refereeing career as he will be the main man behind the whistle for the World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain on Sunday, July 19. However, six years ago, Vincic almost had his career snatched away from him after being arrested during a sex party raid.

Djokovic asks Messi about handling pressure. Argentina star's response is goldAt Fanatics Fest in Manhattan, Novak Djokovic asked Lionel Messi how he handles pressure. The answer from the Argentina superstar won hearts and showed how Messi remained true to himself throughout his footballing journey.

Not Jannik Sinner, Carlos Alcaraz: Novak Djokovic names his biggest rival on courtNovak Djokovic said on CBS Mornings that his biggest rival has always been himself. His remarks also highlighted how ageing, recovery and crowd support are shaping this stage of his career.

Look at Messi: Ex-cricketer explains why India need Ro-Ko at World Cup 2027Mohammed Kaif said India should take Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma to the 2027 ODI World Cup, using Lionel Messi as his example. He argued that experience will matter in South African conditions even as questions persist over Rohit's form and the pair's long-term place in the squad.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
